## Summary

Adds a "Revise now" button to the Evaluation Report header (next to Download Report) that links directly to the Revise Workbench with the correct manuscript and evaluation job IDs. Also improves the empty workbench experience with auto-retry and a Refresh button so users don't see a dead-end on first click.

## Scope

**Touches:** `app/reports/[jobId]/page.tsx` (added `manuscript_id` to Supabase query, added Revise Now link), `components/revision/ReviseWorkbenchClient.tsx` (improved empty workbench UX with auto-retry and refresh button)

**Does NOT touch:** Evaluation pipeline, scoring logic, workbench queue generation, revision findings, or any data model. Read-path only change to report page query (added `manuscript_id` column to existing select).

## Visual Evidence

First render of the Revise Now button on the report header. The button appears inline next to the existing Download Report dropdown, styled as an indigo primary action button matching the existing workbench link style from the evaluate page.

| State | Before | After |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Report header | Download Report button only | "Revise now" button + Download Report button |
| Empty workbench | Static "No live revision queue" message | Auto-retries once after 3s, shows "Building your revision queue..." with Refresh button |

## Accessibility

No new interactive surface beyond standard Link and button elements. Both use semantic HTML with clear text labels.

## Browser Targets

- Chrome latest: standard Link and button elements, no browser-specific concerns
- Safari latest: same
- Firefox latest: same

## Unauthorized Input Sources

None — manuscript_id comes from the existing authenticated Supabase query on the report page. The workbench link passes IDs as query parameters to an already-authenticated page.

## Internal Process Leakage

No internal process details exposed. The Revise Now link uses the same URL pattern as the existing "Open Interactive Revise Queue" button on the evaluate page.

## Input → Action → Output

- Report page loads with completed evaluation → header shows "Revise now" button linking to `/workbench?manuscriptId=X&evaluationJobId=Y` → user clicks → workbench loads revision queue
- If workbench queue is empty on first load (findings still generating) → auto-retries once after 3 seconds → if still empty, shows "Building your revision queue..." with manual Refresh button
- Failure path: if `manuscriptId` is null (shouldn't happen for valid reports), Revise Now button is hidden

## Public-Safe Quality/Status Metrics

No new metrics or status indicators added.

## Runtime/Pipeline Expansion

None — execution surface unchanged. Only added `manuscript_id` to an existing Supabase select on the read path. Auto-retry uses `sessionStorage` to prevent infinite reload loops (retries at most once per session per manuscript/evaluation pair).

## Latency Impact

No measurable impact. Adding one column to an existing query is negligible. The 3-second auto-retry delay is intentional UX for queue generation timing.

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- The auto-retry uses `window.location.reload()` which is a full page reload. This is acceptable for the workbench page which is server-rendered. The `sessionStorage` guard ensures it only retries once per session.
- The "Revise now" button is hidden when `manuscriptId` is null, which should only happen for support-access views or edge cases.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — this PR does not modify lib/evaluation/**, app/api/workers/**, prompts, or any pipeline contract.
